title: Neomammillaria applanata
taxonomy: Neomammillaria applanata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. It is also known as Mammillaria applanata. Neomammillaria applanata is native to Mexico.

intro: Neomammillaria applanata, also known as the pincushion cactus, is a species of succulent plant native to Mexico and Central America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. Neomammillaria applanata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
